name: Río Nora and Viona Greenways Loop
name_original: La Pola Siero, Senda Fluvial del Río Nora, Puente Medieval de Recuna – ruta circular
description: This easy 15.8-mile mountain biking loop along the Río Nora and Viona Greenways offers a scenic ride through nature and history.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Siero, located in Asturias, Spain, offers varied landscapes for cyclists. The region features rolling hills, mountains, and lush oak and pine forests. Trails frequently incorporate natural elements like roots and banked curves, with rivers such as the Río Nora providing picturesque riverside views. Best mountain bike trails around Siero The most popular mountain bike trail is Río Nora and Viona Greenways Loop, an easy 15.8 miles (25.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 54 minutes to complete. This route combines riverside paths with historical elements. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Monte Deva Trail – Monte Deva loop from Lieres, a difficult 47.6 miles (76.6 km) path. This challenging route demands good physical fitness and explores the broader Asturian landscape. Local mountain bikers also love the Nora Riverside Trail loop from Los Corros, a 19.4 miles (31.3 km) trail leading through riverside scenery, often completed in about 2 hours 24 minutes. Mountain biking around Siero is defined by rolling hills, dense forests, and riverside paths. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy greenways to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Siero are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Siero's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Siero?
A: Siero offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 50 routes available for exploration. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy riverside paths to challenging mountain ascents.

Q: Are there easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Siero?
A: Yes, Siero has several easy mountain bike trails perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed ride. One popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1040823292/rio-nora-and-viona-greenways-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Nora and Viona Greenways Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 25.5 km long and combines scenic riverside paths with historical elements. Another easy route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10508866" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto La Madera loop from Viella&lt;/a&gt;, covering 27.8 km.

Q: What are the options for experienced mountain bikers in Siero?
A: Experienced mountain bikers will find challenging routes in Siero that demand good physical fitness. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31904713" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Deva Trail – Monte Deva loop from Lieres&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 76.6 km path that explores the broader Asturian landscape, offering significant elevation changes and technical sections.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Siero?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Siero are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the easy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1040823292/rio-nora-and-viona-greenways-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Nora and Viona Greenways Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31979179" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nora Riverside Trail loop from Los Corros&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect while mountain biking in Siero?
A: Mountain biking in Siero offers diverse Asturian landscapes, including rolling hills, lush oak and pine forests, and picturesque riverside paths along the Río Nora. Trails often feature natural elements like roots and banked curves, providing both scenic beauty and technical interest.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails with good views in Siero?
A: Many trails in Siero offer scenic views, particularly those that ascend into the rolling hills and mountains surrounding the area. The region's natural environment, characterized by dense forests and rivers, provides numerous picturesque vistas throughout your ride.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Siero?
A: The mountain bike trails in Siero are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 40 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the beauty of the Asturian landscape, and the options available for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or attractions near the mountain bike trails in Siero?
A: While focusing on natural beauty, Siero's surroundings also offer points of interest. You can find mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5619300" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Santo Emiliano&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4429467" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de la Madera&lt;/a&gt;. Further afield, near Oviedo, you can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/210761" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sacred Heart of Jesus Monument on Monte Naranco&lt;/a&gt;, which provides cultural context within the beautiful natural setting.

Q: What are the typical conditions for mountain biking in Siero during winter?
A: During winter, some trails in Siero, especially singletrack sections through forests, can become muddy due to rainfall. While many routes remain accessible, it's advisable to check local conditions and trail reports before heading out, and be prepared for potentially wet and slippery surfaces.

Q: Are there any riverside mountain bike trails in Siero?
A: Yes, Siero features several mountain bike trails that follow rivers, offering pleasant riverside views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31979179" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nora Riverside Trail loop from Los Corros&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14990230" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Nora Riverside Trail from Lieres&lt;/a&gt; are excellent examples, providing scenic routes along the Río Nora.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Siero?
A: The best time to mountain bike in Siero is generally during spring and autumn when the weather is milder and the landscapes are particularly vibrant. Summer can also be pleasant, though some days might be warmer. Winter rides are possible but can be muddy, especially after rain.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike routes in Siero?
A: For families, the easier routes, particularly those along greenways and riverside paths, are most suitable. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1040823292/rio-nora-and-viona-greenways-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Nora and Viona Greenways Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice, offering a relatively flat and scenic ride that combines natural beauty with historical elements, making it enjoyable for various ages.
tips: A careful layout that runs along a practically flat itinerary along the banks of the Nora River. The path begins next to the dam of an old mill. After passing a canoe jetty and crossing the road that goes to Valdesoto, we arrive at the Romanón bridge, a beautiful stone bridge that recalls the layout of a Roman road and in the vicinity of which there is a fountain to cool off.

The medieval bridge of Recuna is one of the medieval jewels of the Siero council. It gave access to the malataria, an important hospital institution that, in addition to lepers, was able to care for other patients and walkers. In addition, it is located on the route of one of the six main royal roads: the Camino Real from Villaviciosa to Pola de Siero within the Camino de Santiago.

The area has a fertile territory that produced corn, broad beans, spelt, rye, potatoes, legumes and fruits in abundance. There is a wide presence of flour mills, tanning factories, looms, sausage and chocolate factories. In the 19th century, the discovery and use of coal in its territory also contributed to the economic development of the council.

faqs.question: Is the Río Nora and Viona Greenways Loop suitable for mountain biking?
faqs.answer: While you can ride a mountain bike on this route, the Nora Riverside Trail and Viona Greenways are generally characterized by smooth, well-maintained, and practically flat paths. They are repurposed old railway lines, making them ideal for casual cycling, gravel bikes, or hybrid bikes rather than technical mountain biking. The difficulty is rated as easy.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is mostly smooth and even, following well-maintained fluvial paths and greenways. These paths are designed to be accessible, with gentle gradients. While generally flat, there might be some minor undulations, but nothing overly challenging for cycling.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Río Nora and Viona Greenways Loop?
faqs.answer: The loop starts and ends near La Pola Siero Town Center. You can typically find parking within or on the outskirts of the town, which provides convenient access to the greenways.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The trail is enjoyable for much of the year. Autumn is particularly beautiful, as the riverbanks are adorned with warm golden and coppery tones from the diverse flora. Spring and summer also offer pleasant conditions to enjoy the lush green surroundings.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Río Nora and Viona Greenways Loop?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally allowed on the greenways and fluvial paths. Please ensure your dog is kept on a leash and that you clean up after them to maintain the natural environment for everyone.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee to use the greenways?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits required or entrance fees to access the Río Nora and Viona Greenways Loop. These public paths are freely accessible for everyone to enjoy.

faqs.question: What historical or natural sights can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers a blend of nature and history. You'll cycle alongside the scenic Río Nora with its meanders, through diverse flora like white willow and maple forests, and past historical elements such as the Recuna Medieval Bridge, remnants of old mills, and medieval palaces. Keep an eye out for diverse wintering birds and common frogs.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates significant sections of the Senda fluvial del Río Nora (ciclista) and the Senda fluvial del Río Nora (peatonal), as well as parts of the Vía Verde de la Viona. You'll also briefly intersect with trails like Camin de Careses and Ruta Minera de Jovellanos.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or pubs near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, starting or finishing your loop near La Pola Siero Town Center means you'll have access to various amenities, including cafes, pubs, and other services for refreshments or a meal after your ride.

faqs.question: Is the Río Nora and Viona Greenways Loop suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. The greenways and fluvial paths are designed for accessibility and inclusivity, featuring smooth, gentle terrain that is very suitable for families and children. It's an easy and safe route for a leisurely family bike ride.

faqs.question: What makes this trail unique compared to other routes in the region?
faqs.answer: This loop stands out for its combination of accessible, smooth greenway paths, rich biodiversity along the Río Nora, and numerous historical elements like ancient bridges and mills. It offers a peaceful escape into nature with a cultural touch, making it ideal for recreational cycling and enjoying the Asturian landscape without technical challenges.
